concepts.site_goal: Position Xerox Pressing Co. as a fiercely independent riso-print record label, drive vinyl pre-orders, surface upcoming tour dates, and convert listeners into fans of specific roster artists through a zine-style browsing experience anchored by a persistent audio player bar.
concepts.description: A gritty, photocopied-zine feeling label site for Xerox Pressing Co., a fictional Brooklyn-and-Glasgow indie label specializing in noise rock, post-punk, and experimental electronics. The whole site reads like a stapled fanzine printed at 2am: newsprint cream paper texture, riso pink and electric cyan misregistered ink overlays, oversized black brutalist headlines that often run off the page edge, halftone photos of artists, hand-drawn arrows, paste-up tape marks at corners of cards, marker-scrawl annotations on release covers. A sticky audio player bar pinned to the bottom of every page plays a curated label sampler with track skip, scrubber, and a 'now spinning' marquee. The roster page is an asymmetric grid of artist cards built like trading-card cutouts; the catalog page renders each release as a vinyl-with-sleeve mockup the user can flip to see the B-side. Tour dates live on a typewriter-style list with a sold-out stamp motif. Merch is a contact-sheet grid. The history page is a long scrolling zine spread with column-broken text, footnotes, and inkjet-streak dividers.
concepts.motif: photocopied zine paste-up with misregistered riso ink, halftone artist portraits, marker scrawls, masking-tape corners, and oversized condensed grotesk headlines smashed against a newsprint-cream paper grain

pages.layout_pattern: zine cover splash with oversized misregistered headline, featured release jacket, three latest news pull-quote cards, and an embedded sampler tracklist that loads into the sticky player

sticky audio player bar pinned to bottom

pages.layout_pattern: long scrolling zine spread chaptered by year, with multi-column text, photocopied photographs, pull quotes, footnotes, and an annotated catalog timeline at the bottom
sections: chapter 01 — 2014: a basement and a 4-track
chapter 02 — 2017: the first sold-out pressing
chapter 03 — 2020: pressing plants and pandemics

message_intent: we are loud, independent, and we press our own records
every artist on this roster has a voice worth hearing in full
buying a record here directly supports the band
come see these bands live in a small room near you
this label has a history and a point of view
content_model: Artist: name, hometown, genre tags, bio, photo, linked releases, tour dates, socials
Release: title, artist, catalog number, format (12-inch, 7-inch, cassette, digital), pressing variant, release date, tracklist, sleeve art front/back, audio previews, buy link, liner notes
Tour date: date, city, venue, support acts, ticket link, sold-out flag
Merch item: title, artist (or label), photo set, sizes, price, stock state
Zine entry: chapter title, year, body copy with footnotes, archival photos, pull quotes
Audio track: title, artist, release, duration, file
interactions: sticky bottom audio player bar with play/pause, prev/next, scrubber, volume, now-spinning marquee, and queue popout
click a release sleeve to flip it and read the B-side tracklist
filter roster by genre tag chips (noise, post-punk, ambient, dub, no-wave)
tour list filter by region and 'has tickets left' toggle
merch size selector and add-to-cart pill with cart counter in the header
newsletter signup styled as a tear-off bottom-of-page strip with dashed scissor line
asset_needs: halftone black-and-white artist portrait photos (12-16 artists)
front and back sleeve mockups for ~24 releases
vinyl label center artwork for each release
riso pink and cyan texture overlays (grain, ink splatter, misregistration)
hand-drawn arrow, star, and asterisk SVG marks
audio waveform graphics for player bar
concepts.mobile_behavior: Sticky audio player collapses to a slim bar with cover thumbnail, title marquee, and play toggle. Roster grid stacks into a single column of taped-down cards. Release flip becomes a tap-to-flip card. Tour dates remain a typewriter list. Header navigation collapses into a stamped 'menu' button revealing a full-screen zine-style nav sheet.

concepts.site_goal: Establish Low Tide Tapes as a coastal-DIY cassette-and-vinyl label rooted in West Coast experimental music; convert visitors into mailing-list subscribers, tape-club members, and merch buyers while making the discovery of artists and the back-catalog feel like flipping through a hand-stapled fanzine.
audience: experimental, drone, and lo-fi indie listeners
PNW and West Coast music scene followers
concepts.description: Low Tide Tapes is a fictional Olympia-meets-Oakland micro-label site that leans even harder into the brutalist-zine aesthetic by treating every page like a different page in the same physical zine. The site uses newsprint cream as the base, riso pink and electric cyan as alternating spot inks (each page picks one as the dominant accent), and oversized black wood-type-style display headlines. Photographs are heavy-grain halftone. The signature device is a 'cassette-shell index': the catalog page shows every release as a J-card cassette mockup that opens to reveal the inlay tracklist. The roster page is structured as a contact-sheet of polaroid-style portraits clipped with bulldog clips. The tour page reuses a tide-chart visual metaphor — dates plotted along a wavy horizon line with venue dots. A tape-club subscription page sells a monthly mystery cassette. The history page is laid out like a foldout zine center spread. A sticky bottom audio player bar persists and visually mimics a cassette deck with VU meters that animate to the playing track.
concepts.motif: wood-type brutalist display headlines, bulldog-clipped polaroids, cassette J-card mockups, hand-traced tide line graphics, riso pink and cyan spot inks alternating per page on newsprint cream

pages.layout_pattern: front-of-zine cover with a single oversized wood-type headline, a riso-pink feature spotlight on the newest release, a tide-line strip teasing the next three shows, and an inline tape-club call-out
sections: masthead LOW TIDE TAPES set as huge wood-type with cyan misregistration
issue-number style date line (Vol. XII · Spring 2026)
featured release block: J-card cover, artist, format, edition size, pre-order pill
tide-line strip teasing the next three live shows with venue dots
tape-club call-out card with a join the next mailing button and the next ship date

sticky cassette-deck audio player at the bottom

grid of J-card mockups; click to fold open the inlay with tracklist and credits
edition badge on each (e.g. edition of 150, hand-dubbed)
tape-club promo strip at the bottom

liner essay (zine-style) underneath
more from this artist release strip

message_intent: physical media still matters and we make it by hand
join the tape club to support working artists every month
this is a coastal, slow, deliberate label
discover artists you have not heard anywhere else
come to our basement shows and tape-release nights
content_model: Artist: name, project type (solo / band / duo), region, bio, polaroid portrait, releases, links
Release: catalog number, format (cassette / LP / split / compilation), edition size, J-card art, tracklist, audio previews, buy link, sold-out flag
Tour / show: date, city, venue, performers, doors/show times, ticket source, all-ages flag
Tape-club tier: name, price, frequency, includes, current member count
Merch item: title, sizes, photos, price, stock
Zine essay: title, author, date, body, footnotes
Audio track: title, artist, release, duration
interactions: sticky cassette-deck-style audio player bar at the bottom with animated VU meters, play/pause, skip, scrubber, A/B side toggle
click a J-card to open it and reveal the tracklist on the inlay
drag-to-scroll horizontal tour timeline plotted along a tide-line graphic
tape-club tier selector with a 'next mailing date' countdown
roster filter chips by region (PNW, Bay, SoCal, Cascadia-elsewhere)
zine essay table of contents with anchor jumps and a 'reading time' badge
asset_needs: cassette J-card mockups (front + inlay) for ~30 releases
polaroid-style halftone portrait photos for ~14 artists
wood-type display headline glyph set or close approximation
hand-traced wavy tide-line SVG for the tour page
newsprint cream paper texture and riso ink grain overlays in pink and cyan
bulldog clip and tape decorative graphics
animated VU meter graphics for the player bar
concepts.mobile_behavior: Player bar collapses to a single VU strip with title and play toggle. Cassette J-cards stack vertically and tap-to-open. The tide-line tour timeline rotates to a vertical wave with dates stacked alongside it. Polaroid roster collapses to a single column of clipped cards. Navigation becomes a top sheet styled as a folded zine page.
